title: Free Diffusion
description: Particles spread through a box with no membrane and no electrical forces

title: Diffusion Through a Membrane Pore
description: A membrane separates two compartments and particles can cross only through one pore

title: Pore Permeability
description: Compare a narrow pore with a wide pore to see how permeability changes transport

title: Selective Permeability
description: Two particle types cross different pores, introducing selective permeability

title: Selective Permeability by Probabilistic Gating
description: Hold pore geometry fixed and control cross-membrane flow by ion-specific pore permeability probabilities.

title: Free Diffusion with Electrical Attraction
description: Diffusion in a box with a fixed electrical attractor added

title: What Is a Membrane Potential?
description: Positive and negative ions accumulate on opposite sides of a membrane even before channels are introduced

title: Nernst Equation (One Ion)
description: Connect concentration ratio and electrical potential using the one-ion equilibrium relationship

title: Two-Ion Selective Channels
description: Two ion species with selective pores approach the same equilibrium concentration ratio at different rates

title: Na/K Pump Establishes Gradients
description: Add active pumping to show how real-cell-like Na and K gradients can be created and maintained

title: Goldman Equation from a Charge Wall (2 ions)
description: Selective channels and a charge-wall field produce a full resting-potential lesson with a live comparison trace

title: Free Diffusion with Electrical Attraction
description: Diffusion in a box with a fixed electrical attractor added

title: What Is a Membrane Potential?
description: Positive and negative ions accumulate on opposite sides of a membrane even before channels are introduced

title: Nernst Equation (One Ion)
description: Connect concentration ratio and electrical potential using the one-ion equilibrium relationship

title: Two-Ion Selective Channels
description: Two ion species with selective pores approach the same equilibrium concentration ratio at different rates

title: Na/K Pump Establishes Gradients
description: Add active transport to sustain Na and K gradients while passive selective channels still operate

title: Goldman Equation from Fixed Intracellular Anions (2 ions)
description: Selective channels and a fixed-anion field produce a full resting-potential lesson with a live comparison trace

title: Na/K Pump with Many Stochastic Channels
description: Keep fixed discrete channel locations while each Na and K channel switches open/closed stochastically with Markov dynamics

title: Na/K Pump with Dynamic Charge-Separation Field
description: A fixed-anion field is augmented by a cation-imbalance feedback term so inside/outside charge separation influences transport direction

title: Sub-Threshold Excitation and Inhibition
description: Small perturbations move Vm up or down without triggering a full spike

title: Threshold and All-or-None Spike
description: Crossing threshold shifts permeability and triggers a full action potential response

title: Action Potential Phases (Na In, K Out)
description: A staged visual model links depolarization, repolarization, and recovery to selective pores
